name: Ihringer Winklerberg
description: Warmest vineyard in Germany. Volcanic basalt terroir on steep Kaiserstuhl slopes. Produces powerful Spätburgunder and Grauburgunder.
historicalNotes: Dr. Heger makes benchmark examples. Terraced volcanic slopes provide exceptional heat retention.

name: Oberrotweiler Eichberg
description: Volcanic Kaiserstuhl site producing elegant, structured Spätburgunder. One of Baden's finest.
historicalNotes: Salwey and Bercher make outstanding examples. Name means 'oak mountain.'

name: Achkarrer Schlossberg
description: Steep volcanic slopes producing powerful, complex wines. Premier Kaiserstuhl site.
historicalNotes: Jean-Baptiste Adam, Dr. Heger own prime parcels. Some of steepest vineyards in Baden.

name: Bischoffinger Steinbuck
description: Volcanic Kaiserstuhl vineyard producing structured, mineral Spätburgunder.
historicalNotes: Stigler makes excellent examples from this warm, volcanic site.

name: Durbacher Plauelrain
description: Granite terroir producing elegant, mineral Riesling and Spätburgunder. Unique in Baden for Riesling excellence.
historicalNotes: Huber and Schloss Staufenberg make benchmark wines. Granite soil rare in Baden.

name: Neuweirer Mauerberg
description: Granite slopes producing taut, mineral Riesling. Best Riesling site in Baden.
historicalNotes: Schloss Neuweier makes outstanding dry Riesling from ancient granite.

name: Staufener Schlossberg
description: Mixed volcanic and limestone terroir producing complex Spätburgunder.
historicalNotes: Historic site with castle ruins overlooking vineyards.

name: Burkheimer Feuerberg
description: Warm volcanic site producing ripe, structured Spätburgunder. Name means 'fire mountain.'
historicalNotes: Bercher makes excellent examples. One of the warmest sites in Kaiserstuhl.
